dependenciesInterpreter.name: Python interpreter
dependenciesInterpreter.description: Python interpreter path to use for dependencies installation. Only required for special packages that need to be compiled from source. Must be python 3.9.

condaRuntime.name: Conda runtime
condaRuntime.description: Conda runtime path to use for environment management. This is used to create and manage conda environments for the application.

showPlacedID.name: Show placedIDs
showPlacedID.description: Whether to show the placedID of placed blocks in the flow builder.

arrowLook.name: Arrow appareance
arrowLook.description: Choose how the connections between blocks should look like.

developmentMode.name: Development mode
developmentMode.description: Development mode enables the developer tools in the application, such as plugin reloading. This setting requires the app to be reloaded.

pluginsFolders.name: Plugin directories
pluginsFolders.description: Add additional folders where Horus should look for plugins. Requires a restart of Horus.

extensionDevelopmentURL.name: Extension URL
extensionDevelopmentURL.description: When opening the extension 'Development', the URL to use in the iframe. Remember to disable CORS in your browser.

molstarHidden.name: Mol* hidden
molstarHidden.description: Whether to start the app with the Mol* panel initialized or not. This can improve the performance of the application.

disableMolstar.name: Disable Mol*
disableMolstar.description: If you are having trouble loading flows with huge structures you can disable Mol* entirely.

queueWaitTime.name: Remote check interval
queueWaitTime.description: The interval in seconds to check for remote jobs status (in seconds).

collapseBlocks.name: Collapse blocks
collapseBlocks.description: Collapse the block registry panel by default.

runFlowsInSlurm.name: Run flows in SLURM
runFlowsInSlurm.description: If true, the entire flow will be sent as a slurm job instead of a subprocess.

slurmScript.name: Submission script
slurmScript.description: The script that Horus will use to submit locally the flows. The command to run the flow will be automatically appended at the end.
